title: Christine Lagarde on Why Consensus Isn’t Always the Right Approach

transcript.text: How do you find consensus at the
governing council level? I mean, is it
right actually to always try and find
consensus in in terms of just mechanics?
>> You know, not not always. There there
are times and there are, I'm sure,
circumstances or sectors where you have
to just drive through and and be this
authoritative uh leader who is going to
carry the day and ignore completely what
the views of others are. It's just not
my style. And so far, it has it has
served me reasonably well. Even in times
of crisis, when you've established your
leadership, notably by respecting the
views, notably by being tolerant,
notably by listening to what people have
to say in times of crisis, when you have
to move fast, then people will go with
you and they will respect you.
>> This is what about values? Is it your
your value system? Is that how you want
to lead because of of what you think is
right and just or or does it deliver
just better results?
>> I think it's rooted in my values and in
my personal background. Yes.
